title: Fix the malformed codex advisor + codex.review commands, and bring them under AO8 (read-only)
action: Two live defects in one command string, both measured. (1) `models.claude.advisor.command` and `models.codex.review.command` both end in a BARE positional `workspace-write`. Per `codex exec --help`, the bare positional IS the prompt ('[PROMPT] Initial instructions for the agent'), and the sandbox flag is `-s/--sandbox`. So both commands have been passing the literal string 'workspace-write' as their question and never reading stdin — the external path has likely never worked. (2) `--yolo` is codex's alias for `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`, whose own help says 'execute commands WITHOUT SANDBOXING. EXTREMELY DANGEROUS.' So the sandbox the owner believed was on has never been on. Fix both to the form PROVEN to work this session (exit 0, correct answer, read-only): `codex exec -m <model> -s read-only -c model_reasoning_effort="high" -`. The trailing `-` is what makes codex read the prompt from stdin (AO19) — it is load-bearing, not decoration. `-s read-only` also satisfies AO8 (advice-class slots run read-only) directly rather than deferring it: an advisor gives advice; it needs no write access. Then remove `--dangerously-skip-permissions` from the generation preset in `.bee/config-sample-cli-executors.json` and correct the recommendation in `docs/model-presets.md` — bee must not SHIP a machine-wide bypass as its copy-paste default while `swarming-reference.md:91` forbids exactly that.

truths: Both codex commands carry -s read-only and a trailing - (stdin prompt transport)
No bee-shipped config or doc recommends --yolo or --dangerously-skip-permissions as a default
The advisor is proven by a REAL run answering a REAL repo question, not by assertion
AO8 (advice-class slots run read-only) is satisfied, not deferred
prohibitions: Do NOT keep --yolo on any advice-class slot
Do NOT drop the trailing - : without it codex never reads the prompt (AO19)
Do NOT touch models.claude.generation in this cell — the cli-generation work is the re-planned Slice 2A

verify: ! grep -q 'yolo\|dangerously-skip-permissions' .bee/config.json .bee/config-sample-cli-executors.json && python3 -c "import json,sys; c=json.load(open('.bee/config.json')); a=c['models']['claude']['advisor']['command']; r=c['models']['codex']['review']['command']; assert '-s read-only' in a and a.rstrip().endswith('-'), a; assert '-s read-only' in r and r.rstrip().endswith('-'), r; print('CONFIG_OK')" && printf 'Answer in one short sentence only. In this repo, what two tool names are in the DISPATCH_TOOLS constant in hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs?' | timeout 240 codex exec -m gpt-5.6-sol -s read-only -c model_reasoning_effort="high" - 2>&1 | grep -qi 'Agent' && echo ADVISOR_LIVE

trace.verification_evidence:
CONFIG_OK
ADVISOR_LIVE
cell verify exit=0

Full chain: test_lib && test_onboard_bee && test_portable_paths && test_model_guard && test_write_guard && test_hook_contracts
FULL VERIFY exit=0 -- 1011 PASS, 0 failed, ALL PASS

Live advisor proof (FIRST ever end-to-end run of bee's external advisor path; closes decision 0019's "first dogfood pending", confidence 0.6):
  cmd:   codex exec -m gpt-5.6-sol -s read-only -c model_reasoning_effort="high" -
  stdin: what two tool names are in the DISPATCH_TOOLS constant in hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs?
  reply: "It contains `Agent` and `Task`."   <- matches hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs:29 exactly
  exit:  0    sandbox: read-only    no --yolo needed

agy without the bypass flag (proof the dangerous flag was never needed for a gather):
  cmd:   bash -lc 'agy -p "$(cat)" --model "Gemini 3.5 Flash (High)" --sandbox --mode plan --print-timeout 10m'
  reply: correctly cited hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs#L29 -> Agent and Task
  exit:  0

Root cause fixed: `workspace-write` was a BARE POSITIONAL, and per `codex exec --help` the bare positional IS the prompt. The -s flag was missing, and --yolo (= --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox) meant no sandbox ran at all.
